messages.content:
Produce your very own perspective on the given topic. Align tone and examples with the persona's background and traits. Focus on discussing your topic. Avoid bullet points. Produce continuous prose. Write at least 250 words, up to 500 words.

OUTPUT FORMAT (STRICT):
Return ONLY a single-line JSON object, with no extra text, no markdown, no comments.
Here is the required structure example (values are placeholders):

response_raw:
{
"mode": "human",
"topic": "How to manage and dealing with fake content on social media.",
"opinion": "To manage and deal with fake content on social media, it is important to be aware of the potential sources of misinformation and take steps to verify the accuracy of posts before sharing them. This can include fact-checking websites and using reverse image search tools to identify manipulated or altered images. Additionally, setting your social media platforms to notify you when a post has been flagged as false or misleading can help you avoid spreading misinformation unintentionally. Furthermore, engaging in open and respectful conversations with others on social media can help to build trust and promote a more informed online community. Ultimately, it is up to each individual user to take an active role in managing and dealing with fake content on social media."

response_parsed.opinion: To manage and deal with fake content on social media, it is important to be aware of the potential sources of misinformation and take steps to verify the accuracy of posts before sharing them. This can include fact-checking websites and using reverse image search tools to identify manipulated or altered images. Additionally, setting your social media platforms to notify you when a post has been flagged as false or misleading can help you avoid spreading misinformation unintentionally. Furthermore, engaging in open and respectful conversations with others on social media can help to build trust and promote a more informed online community. Ultimately, it is up to each individual user to take an active role in managing and dealing with fake content on social media.

This can include fact-checking websites and using reverse image search tools to identify manipulated or altered images. Additionally, setting your social media platforms to notify you when a post has been flagged as false or misleading can help you avoid spreading misinformation unintentionally.
